extraction.one_line_summary: DE GENDT Thomas attacked from breakaway before final tunnel, held 23-27 second gap over fractured chase despite Movistar/Mitchelton pressure, took stage and race lead
tactical_patterns: De Gendt controlled breakaway psychologically, making companions believe in collective result before attacking solo pre-tunnel
Movistar/UAE initiated softening attacks in tunnel approach but coordination failed, allowing De Gendt to maintain 24-second buffer
QUINTANA attacked YATES into headwind immediately after climb top, easily neutralized showing poor terrain selection for lightweight climber
Chase group's gap oscillated 19-27 seconds in final 1700m due to intermittent cooperation, never sustaining coordinated pursuit

rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: positive: held power output constant through tunnel/no-rest parcours where others expected fatigue collapse, gaining race lead
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: appeared 'elbows out in happy place' during tunnel attacks, suggesting better form than expected
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: easily neutralized Quintana's headwind attack, then won chase group sprint for minor time bonuses
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: tactical error attacking lightweight climber into headwind post-climb, wasted energy before finish
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: neutral: designated Mitchelton-Scott leader but team control insufficient to reel in 23-second deficit
